sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Madrid come into this match with a strong run of form, winning 3 of their last 5 matches, including a solid 2-0 victory over Sevilla. Real Betis, on the other hand, are unbeaten in their last 4 games, with a standout 4-0 win against Getafe and no goals conceded in their last 3 matches, showing defensive solidity.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, results have been split, with Real Madrid winning 2-0 at home in September 2024, while Real Betis secured a 2-1 victory in their most recent clash in March 2025. Historically, matches between these sides often see under 2.5 goals, indicating tight, low-scoring affairs.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Madrid, sitting 2nd in La Liga with 42 points, boast a strong attack (36 goals, 2nd in the league) and the best defense (16 goals conceded). Real Betis, in 6th with 28 points, have scored 29 goals but face a tough challenge against Madrid's defensive record, despite their recent clean sheets.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Real Madrid will miss Kylian Mbappé, a significant loss given his 18 goals this season, but players like Vinícius Júnior (5 goals, 5 assists) remain a threat. Real Betis rely on Cucho Hernández (7 goals) for firepower, with their squad largely intact for this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Real Madrid's superior league standing, home advantage at the Bernabéu, and Sofascore's 60% probability for a home win, they are favored to win. However, Betis' recent defensive resilience could make it a close contest, likely resulting in a narrow victory for Madrid.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Real Madrid holds a narrow 1-0 lead over Real Betis at the Bernabéu. The home side struck in the 20th minute through a well-taken header by Gonzalo García, capitalizing on their dominance in the first half. Real Madrid has controlled possession and created more dangerous opportunities, while Real Betis has struggled to generate clear-cut chances despite showing some resilience defensively. The away side will need to find a way to penetrate Madrid's defense if they hope to get back into the game.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid started the game on the front foot with early attacking intent, maintaining consistent pressure through prolonged spells of dominance in the first half. Their momentum peaked around the time of the goal, showcasing a flurry of attacking moves that kept Betis on the back foot. While Betis had brief moments of momentum, particularly in patches towards the middle and end of the half, they failed to convert these into significant threats. Madrid's attacking rhythm dipped slightly towards the latter stages, but they remained the more dangerous side overall.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Real Madrid, Gonzalo García has been the standout player, not only for his goal but also for his presence in the final third. Rodrygo played a crucial role in setting up the goal, showing creativity and vision in attack. Jude Bellingham has also been influential in midfield with key contributions in both defense and attack. On the Real Betis side, Cucho Hernández has shown glimpses of danger upfront, while players like Aitor Ruibal and Angel Ortiz have tried to stabilize the team defensively despite the pressure from Madrid.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid's control of the ball and ability to enter the final third frequently have been key to their first-half performance. Their midfield, led by players like Bellingham and Camavinga, has dictated the tempo and stifled Betis' attempts to build play. On the other hand, Real Betis has relied on a more defensive structure, looking to exploit counter-attacks, but their lack of precision in the final third has let them down. They’ve shown strength in aerial duels, which could be a potential avenue to explore in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Real Madrid is likely to continue their high-pressing game and look to extend their lead early on. They have the attacking talent to punish any defensive lapses from Betis. Meanwhile, Betis will need to take more risks if they are to equalize, potentially opening up spaces for Madrid to exploit on the break. Substitutions could play a significant role, especially for Betis, who may need fresh legs to inject energy into their attack. Expect Madrid to remain the dominant force, but Betis could pose a threat if they capitalize on set pieces or quick transitions.
